title: Maersk CEO: 'In Asia and the Middle East, our fuel supply points risk running dry'
subhead: The head of the Danish maritime transport company tells Le Monde how the war in the Middle East has forced the world's second-largest shipping line to deploy new sea routes. Ten of the company's ships are currently stranded in the Persian Gulf.

title: Hungarian PM Orban campaigns on fears of war and hatred of Ukraine
subhead: Threatened in the polls for the April parliamentary elections by Peter Magyar's party, nationalist Prime Minister Viktor Orban has portrayed his rival as an agent who serves Kyiv and the European Union.

title: Far right lays trap for Dati in Paris mayoral race
subhead: Rachida Dati, the conservative candidate for mayor of Paris, finds herself in an uncomfortable position as Eric Zemmour's party calls for an alliance in the second round.

title: PSG is struggling, but not yet out of the running
subhead: Paris Saint-Germain, which faces Chelsea on Wednesday in the round of 16 of the Champions League, has lost the dominance it displayed last season. Nevertheless, the current campaign is far from over, writes journalist Jérôme Latta.

title: In Dubai, Iranian entrepreneurs are caught in the crossfire: 'Iran has become a black hole'
subhead: Long established in the financial stronghold of the United Arab Emirates, the community now fears reprisals against relatives who remain in Iran.

The ghosts of the new Chilean president's family
subhead: José Antonio Kast, who will be sworn in on Wednesday, makes no secret of his admiration for Augusto Pinochet's regime. He is less forthcoming about his father's past as a former Wehrmacht soldier and his family's role during the atrocities committed in Paine during the 1973 coup.

title: Trump seeks way out of Iran war but struggles to define it
subhead: Speaking from Florida on Monday, the US president highlighted Washington's military successes but failed to offer a clear path out of the conflict, fueling the impression of ongoing improvisation.

title: The United Arab Emirates is caught in the fog of war
subhead: Surprised by the scale of Iran's attacks on their territory, Emirati leaders have worked to downplay the impact in order to retain the confidence of investors and tourists, while continuing to place orders for weapons.
